Optus restores nbn services across Brisbane, parts of Queensland, website shows
data.description:
Dec 4 (Reuters) - Australian telecom company Optus has fully restored its National Broadband Network (nbn) services in Brisbane and other affected areas of Queensland following a recent outage, according to an update on its website on Thursday.

Optus, a company owned by Singapore Telecommunications, did not immediately respond to a Reuters' request for comment.

(Reporting by Roshan Thomas in Bengaluru; Editing by Sherry Jacob-Phillips)

data.title: Snowflake's product revenue outlook falls short of investor ambition, shares fall
data.description:
Dec 3 (Reuters) - Snowflake forecast fourth-quarter product revenue above analysts' estimates but falls short of lofty investor expectations for stronger growth, sending its shares down about 8% in extended trading.

The cloud data analytics firm, whose shares have surged more than 70% this year, expects product revenue of $1.19 billion to $1.20 billion for the fourth quarter, representing 27% growth. The forecast was above analysts' estimates of $1.18 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

"Given the dramatic appreciation in share price this year, investors were expecting guidance of more than 30%. We believe this growth will materialize next quarter allowing shares to continue appreciating," said D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria.

Snowflake expanded its partnerships to strengthen its agentic AI and cloud ecosystem, announcing a multi-year $200 million agreement with Anthropic to bring Claude models to its platform for advanced reasoning and secure multi-step analysis.

The company also deepened ties with Accenture to help enterprises scale generative AI innovation, and unveiled new integrations with Amazon Web Services after surpassing $2 billion in sales on the AWS Marketplace this year.

The Bozeman, Montana-based company is similarly working with Google's Gemini AI and other partners to embed large language models into its tools so customers can build and run AI applications directly on Snowflake-managed data.

The push comes as businesses accelerate generative AI strategies, using large language models for analytics, automation and customer engagement thereby fueling demand for AI-enabled cloud services across sectors, benefiting companies like Snowflake.

The company reported quarterly revenue of $1.21 billion, beating analysts' estimates of $1.18 billion.

(Reporting by Kritika Lamba in Bengaluru; Editing by Krishna Chandra Eluri)

By Kritika Lamba

data.title: Latam Airlines expects 2026 revenues at $15.5 billion to $16 billion
Venezuela's Maduro says call with Trump was 'respectful and cordial'
data.description:
Dec 3 (Reuters) - Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro confirmed on Wednesday that he had a "respectful and cordial" conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump about 10 days ago.

Maduro also said that steps were being taken toward a respectful dialogue between the two countries.

(Reporting by Reuters Staff; Editing by Leslie Adler)

data.title: US stocks close higher as data keeps Fed rate cut hopes elevated
data.description:
STORY: Wall Street's main indexes closed higher on Wednesday, with the Dow adding almost nine-tenths of a percent, the S&P 500 gaining three-tenths of a percent and the Nasdaq ticking up marginally.

The ADP National Employment Report showed U.S. private payrolls unexpectedly declined in November, helping to keep expectations elevated for an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week.

But Dean Smith, chief strategist and portfolio manager at FolioBeyond, believes a rate cut would be a step backwards in the battle against rising prices.

"I think there's a pretty broad consensus that conditions in the labor market are softening. The Federal Reserve has turned their attention to that. Personally, I think that's a mistake. I think inflation should continue to be their focus. We're still running around 3%, which is 50% higher than their stated 2% target. And they're probably going to cut anyway. I think with the benefit of hindsight, we're going to look back and say they should have stood pat."

Stocks on the move Wednesday included Microsoft, which shed 2.5% after a CNBC report said the tech giant has cut AI software sales quotas after many salespeople at the company missed their targets. Microsoft denied the report.

Shares of Marvell Technology jumped almost 8% after the chipmaker said it will buy semiconductor startup Celestial AI in a deal worth $3.25 billion.

And shares of American Eagle Outfitters surged 15% after the retailer raised its annual comparable sales forecast, betting on strong demand during the holiday season.

data.description:

"Trump Administration Cancels Energy Grants in Mostly Blue States -- WSJ," at 2:09 p.m. ET on Oct. 2, incorrectly said it was expected to get $4 million.

data.title: Utilities Down on Cyclical Bias - Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers ticked down as traders rotated into higher risk sectors, betting weak jobs data would spur the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

The SPDR Select Sector Utilities exchange-traded fund, which tracks the utilities industry group of the S&P 500, fell 0.3%, but remains roughly 15% higher for the year to date.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1737ET

data.title: Communications Services Up on Deal Bets - Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies rose amid deal intrigue.

Warner Bros Discovery continued to inch up as details of interested parties' bids for the media conglomerate emerged.

Comcast, for example, aims to roll the Hollywood studio and its streaming service in with its NBCUniversal unit.

The widow of telecommunications tycoon and Global Crossing cofounder Gary Winnick is battling lenders over her deceased husband's debts.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1737ET

data.title: Tech Gains Limited Amid Microsoft AI Fears - Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies rose, but not by as much as the broad market, amid fears about Microsoft's artificial-intelligence profits.

Shares of the software giant and OpenAI affiliate fell after a report from tech-focused site The Information that the Seattle giant was easing sales quotas for enterprise AI products such as Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Amazon.com is facing a new labor challenge, this time from small-business owners who run the company's package-delivery network, who are seeking higher pay.

Marvell Technologies rose sharply afer the chip maker posted strong earnings growth, and said it would be acquiring AI start-up Celestial AI in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $3.25 billion.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1737ET

data.title: Financials Up on Rate Bets - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose sharply as traders bet weak jobs data would spur a Federal Reserve interest-rate cut.

Another Fed rate cut could drive borrowing rates, including mortgage rates to attractive levels around 6%, and increase demand for loans.

The Trump administration canceled a slate of interviews set to start this week with a group of finalists to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve as President Trump again suggested he had made up his mind about who should lead the central bank.

Royal Bank of Canada its dividend 6.5% after logging record net income in the final quarter of its fiscal year with higher earnings across most of its business segments.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1737ET

data.title: Consumer Cos Up After Mixed Earnings - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rose after mixed earnings, amid hopes for a rate cut.

Macy's boosted its sales projection, but shares fell slightly as the department-store chain warned its customers were becoming choosier, even as they continued to shop.

Deep discounter Dollar Tree shares rose after it said more shoppers were flocking to its stores hunting for low-cost goods.

Shares of teen retailer American Eagle Outfitters rose after it posted record sales and better-than-expected quarterly earnings, as marketing partnerships with Sydney Sweeney and Travis Kelce paid off.

Shares of Spanish Zara owner Inditex climbed after strong Black Friday sales performance bolstered its prospects.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1736ET

data.title: Health Care Up on Alzheimer's Hopes - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies rose after one potential breakthrough on a brain disease.

Bristol Myers Squibb rallied after the biopharmaceutical company said that it was adding patients for its study on its drug, Cobenfy, to treat psychosis in patients with Alzheimer's disease.

Shares of Eisai, which produces Alzheimer's treatment Leqembi in collaboration with Biogen, fell slightly.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1736ET

data.title: Industrials Up on Rate Cut Bets - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies rose as weak employment survey spurred bets on an imminent rate cut.

Private employers shed 32,000 jobs in November, ADP estimated, a swing from the 47,000 private-sector jobs that ADP estimated were added in October. Analysts were expecting to see 40,000 new private-sector jobs.

"Small businesses are especially feeling the pressure from trade uncertainty," said LPL Financial Chief Economist Jeffrey Roach. "Small businesses cut jobs the most since May 2020."

European aerospace giant Airbus said it was lowering its aircraft delivery target for the year because of a quality issue with metal panels on hundreds of A320 jets, a blow for the company as it struggles to overcome supply-chain hurdles.

President Trump said he plans to roll back federal fuel-economy rules for passenger vehicles, his latest move to relieve the U.S. auto industry from mandates to build cleaner, more-fuel efficient cars.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1736ET

data.title: Materials Up as Dollar Falls Against Rivals - Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials rose as the dollar fell against rivals due to interest-rate calculations.

The price of copper hit a record high on the London Mercantile Exchange, reflecting hedging against U.S. sanctions.

In the U.S., copper futures rose 2.9% to $5.31 a pound, still 8.4% below July highs. Silver futures closed just shy of an all-time high, above $57 an ounce, more than double their value at the start of the year.

Metals trading and mining giant Glencore said it would restart its Alumbrera copper mine in Argentina as its targets around 1.6 million metric tons of copper production a year by 2035.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-03-25 1736ET

US backs UN demand for Russia to return abducted Ukrainian children
data.description:
UNITED NATIONS, Dec 3 (Reuters) - The United States joined 90 other countries at the United Nations on Wednesday to demand Russia "ensure the immediate, safe and unconditional return of all Ukrainian children who have been forcibly transferred or deported" and urge Moscow to stop the practice.

The adoption of the resolution by the 193-member General Assembly came a day after U.S. President Donald Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in the Kremlin for talks on how to end the deadliest European conflict since World War Two. 

Russia urged countries to oppose the U.N. resolution, which was drafted by Ukraine, Canada and the European Union. 

"Each vote for the resolution is support for lies, war and confrontation. Every vote against is a vote for peace," Russia's Deputy U.N. Ambassador Maria Zabolotskaya told the General Assembly ahead of the vote. 

Russia and 11 other countries voted against the resolution, while 57 countries abstained. General Assembly resolutions are not binding but carry weight as a reflection of the global view.

Russia invaded Ukraine in February 2022. Ukraine accuses Russia of abducting tens of thousands of its children to Russia or Russian-occupied territory without the consent of family or guardians. Previously Moscow has said it has been protecting vulnerable children from a war zone.

U.S. first lady Melania Trump has made the repatriation of Ukrainian children from Russia a priority. In October she said that she had secured an open line of communication with Putin and that some children had been returned to their families with more to be reunited soon.

The International Criminal Court issued an arrest warrant in March 2023 for Putin, accusing him of the war crime of illegally deporting hundreds of children from Ukraine. Russia denounced the move as "outrageous and unacceptable."

(Reporting by Michelle Nichols; Editing by Lincoln Feast)

By Michelle Nichols

Morgan Stanley exec expects broad surge in M&A
data.description:
NEW YORK, Dec 3 (Reuters) - Morgan Stanley is seeing a strong deal pipeline next year for mergers, acquisitions and IPOs, its co-head of investment banking, Mo Assomull, said on Wednesday.

M&A pipelines are broadly healthy across sectors, with technology, healthcare, industrials and financials being particularly active, he said on a panel at the Reuters NEXT conference in New York. The economic backdrop for deals is also supportive, he added. 

The Trump administration's pro-growth stance is also expected to spur consolidation in the financial sector, Assomull said.

"We do expect to have more banks-related M&A over the next few years, for many reasons," he said. Those reasons include a more friendly regulatory environment and lenders' need to increase their scale.

Regarding artificial intelligence, investors and lenders have become more "discerning" about financing companies in the space, he said.

Morgan Stanley's profit beat estimates in the third quarter as a surge in dealmaking drove revenue to records. The bank's finance chief said in October that its investment banking pipeline was at "all-time highs."

View the live broadcast of the World Stage here and read full coverage here.

(Reporting by Lananh Nguyen and Tatiana Bautzer in New York; Editing by Chris Reese and Matthew Lewis)

By Tatiana Bautzer and Lananh Nguyen
